Am I In Heaven? contained Alter Me and Cellophane teases and quotes, Rattlesnake, Hot Water, and Head On/Pill teases, and a Sea Of Trees jam with lyrics. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. Alter Me I through Am I In Heaven? were confirmed by the band's Facebook livestream. The remaining songs were confirmed through contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlist@kglw.net.

High Voltage (AC/DC) played before and as the band walked on stage for the first set. All Is Known was introduced as a new song and contained alternate lyrics. During Billabong Valley, the guitar strap fell off the Flying Microtonal Banana while Ambrose was using it, with Joey jokingly telling Stu afterwards that his guitar was broken and that Ambrose should "stick to the blues harp". NOBODY'S BABY (Sheer Mag) played before and as the band walked on stage for the second set. Before Gamma Knife, the crowd started chanting for Stu to do a "shoey", to which he told them to "be reasonable" and that he was "too old for that". The Balrog was introduced as a new song. Am I In Heaven? contained an intro jam with Head On/Pill teases and a medley of teases and quotes including Alter Me, Cellophane, Rattlesnake, Hot Water, Head On/Pill and The River as well as a Sea of Trees jam with lyrics. This performance was filmed in 360-degree video and released as "King Gizzard & The Lizard Wizard - 360° Full Concert" on the Banalarama YouTube channel, featuring audio mixed by Stu

This show featured the debut of Vomit Coffin. Following Rattlesnake, the band wished a happy birthday to Eric. Am I In Heaven? contained teases and quotes of Cellophane, The River, Head On/Pill, I'm In Your Mind, Sea of Trees, Rattlesnake, and Robot Stop. Prior to the show, Stu accidentally posted a link containing demos for all of Polygondwanaland to the Facebook fan page under the name “Blinky Bill”. Although removed shortly after, someone was able to download the first two demos causing Stu to message them claiming he was a fan named Han-Tyumi and asked for the demos not to be reposted. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete, but no recordings are available. KGLW.net staff believes the setlist is complete based on contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlist@kglw.net.
